---
title: "invgamma_prior"
generated: true
description: "Build an inverse-gamma lengthscale prior that penalises small `rho`."
---

[‹ back to `nitrix.stats.priors`](../nitrix-stats-priors.qmd)

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:invgamma_prior -->
```python
invgamma_prior(a: float, b: float) -> PriorFn
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:invgamma_prior -->
Build an inverse-gamma lengthscale prior that penalises small `rho`.

The prior density is
$p(\rho) \propto \rho^{-a-1} \exp(-b / \rho)$ on $\rho > 0$,
so the returned penalty is
$-\log p(\rho) = (a + 1) \log \rho + b / \rho$ (up to an additive
constant).  The $b / \rho$ term diverges as $\rho \to 0$, so
this pulls the estimate up, away from a lengthscale that collapses into
noise-fitting -- the usual penalised-complexity-style regulariser for a
weakly-identified $\rho$ (e.g. sparse or short series).
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:invgamma_prior -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|------------|--------|--------|----------|--------------------------------------------------------------|
| `a` | `float` | — | — | Shape parameter $a$ (must be positive). |
| `b` | `float` | — | — | Scale parameter $b$ (must be positive). Together with `a` it sets the prior mode $b / (a + 1)$; pick `a` and `b` so that is a plausibly-small lengthscale. |

**Returns** `PriorFn`. A callable mapping a scalar lengthscale $\rho$ (shape `()`) to the scalar negative log-density $-\log p(\rho)$ (shape `()`), suitable for the `map_rho=` argument of `nitrix.stats.gp_fit` and `nitrix.stats.hgp_fit`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->
